'different function signatures' % implementation)\n98: \n99:     if implementation_spec.defaults is not None:\n100:         if dispatcher_spec.defaults != (None,) * len(dispatcher_spec.defaults):\n101:             raise RuntimeError('dispatcher functions can only use None for '\n102:                                'default argument values')\n103: \n104: \n105: def array_function_dispatch(dispatcher=None, module=None, verify=True,\n106:                             docs_from_dispatcher=False):\n107:     \"\"\"Decorator for adding dispatch with the __array_function__ protocol.\n108: \n109:     See NEP-18 for example usage.\n110: \n111:     Parameters\n112:     ----------\n113:     dispatcher : callable or None\n114:         Function that when called like ``dispatcher(*args, **kwargs)`` with\n115:         arguments from the NumPy function call returns an iterable of\n116:         array-like arguments to check for ``__array_function__``.\n117: \n118:         If `None`, the first argument is used as the single `like=` argument\n119:         and not passed on.  A function implementing `like=` must call its\n120:         dispatcher with `like` as the first non-keyword argument.\n121:     module : str, optional\n122:         __module__ attribute to set on new function, e.g., ``module='numpy'``.\n123:         By default, module is copied from the decorated function.\n124:     verify : bool, optional\n125:         If True, verify the that the signature of the dispatcher and decorated\n126:         function signatures match exactly: all required and optional arguments\n127:         should appear in order with the same names, but the default values for\n128:         all optional arguments should be ``None``. Only disable verification\n129:         if the dispatcher's signature needs to deviate for some particular\n130:         reason, e.g., because the function has a signature like\n131:         ``func(*args, **kwargs)``.\n132:     docs_from_dispatcher : bool, optional\n133:         If True, copy docs from the dispatcher function onto the dispatched\n134:         function, rather than from the implementation. This is useful for\n135:         functions defined in C, which otherwise don't have docstrings.\n136: \n137:     Returns\n138:     -------\n139:     Function suitable for decorating the implementation of a NumPy function.\n140: \n141:     \"\"\"\n142:     def decorator(implementation):\n143:         if verify:\n144:             if dispatcher is not None:\n145:                 verify_matching_signatures(implementation, dispatcher)\n146:             else:\n147:                 # Using __code__ directly similar to verify_matching_signature\n148:                 co = implementation.__code__\n149:                 last_arg = co.co_argcount + co.co_kwonlyargcount - 1\n150:                 last_arg = co.co_varnames[last_arg]\n151:                 if last_arg != \"like\" or co.co_kwonlyargcount == 0:\n152:                     raise RuntimeError(\n153:                         \"__array_function__ expects `like=` to be the last \"\n154:                         \"argument and a keyword-only argument. \"\n155:                         f\"{implementation} does not seem to comply.\")\n156: \n157:         if docs_from_dispatcher:\n158:             add_docstring(implementation, dispatcher.__doc__)\n159: \n160:         public_api = _ArrayFunctionDispatcher(dispatcher, implementation)\n161:         public_api = functools.wraps(implementation)(public_api)\n162: \n163:         if module is not None:\n164:             public_api.__module__ = module\n165: \n166:         ARRAY_FUNCTIONS.add(public_api)\n167: \n168:         return public_api\n169: \n170:     return decorator\n171: \n172: \n173: def array_function_from_dispatcher(\n174:         implementation, module=None, verify=True, docs_from_dispatcher=True):\n175:     \"\"\"Like array_function_dispatcher, but with function arguments flipped.\"\"\"\n176: \n177:     def decorator(dispatcher):\n178:         return array_function_dispatch(\n179:             dispatcher, module, verify=verify,\n180:             docs_from_dispatcher=docs_from_dispatcher)(implementation)\n181:     return decorator\n182: ",

9. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "mkdir -p numpy/_core numpy/lib numpy/linalg numpy/fft benchmarks/benchmarks doc/neps",
  "description": "Create directory structure for modified files"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,

10. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "\"\"\"Implementation of __array_function__ overrides from NEP-18.\"\"\"\nimport collections\nimport functools\n\nfrom .._utils import set_module\nfrom .._utils._inspect import getargspec\nfrom numpy._core._multiarray_umath import (\n    add_docstring,  _get_implementing_args, _ArrayFunctionDispatcher)\n\n\nARRAY_FUNCTIONS = set()\n\narray_function_like_doc = (\n    \"\"\"like : array_like, optional\n        Reference object to allow the creation of arrays which are not\n        NumPy arrays. If an array-like passed in as ``like`` supports\n        the ``__array_function__`` protocol, the result will be defined\n        by it. In this case, it ensures the creation of an array object\n        compatible with that passed in via this argument.\"\"\"\n)\n\ndef get_array_function_like_doc(public_api, docstring_template=\"\"):\n    ARRAY_FUNCTIONS.add(public_api)\n    docstring = public_api.__doc__ or docstring_template\n    return docstring.replace(\"${ARRAY_FUNCTION_LIKE}\", array_function_like_doc)\n\ndef finalize_array_function_like(public_api):\n    public_api.__doc__ = get_array_function_like_doc(public_api)\n    return public_api\n\n\nadd_docstring(\n    _ArrayFunctionDispatcher,\n    \"\"\"\n    Class to wrap functions with checks for __array_function__ overrides.\n\n    All arguments are required, and can only be passed by position.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    dispatcher : function or None\n        The dispatcher function that returns a single sequence-like object\n        of all arguments relevant.  It must have the same signature (except\n        the default values) as the actual implementation.\n        If ``None``, this is a ``like=`` dispatcher and the\n        ``_ArrayFunctionDispatcher`` must be called with ``like`` as the\n        first (additional and positional) argument.\n    implementation : function\n        Function that implements the operation on NumPy arrays without\n        overrides.  Arguments passed calling the ``_ArrayFunctionDispatcher``\n        will be forwarded to this (and the ``dispatcher``) as if using\n        ``*args, **kwargs``.\n\n    Attributes\n    ----------\n    _implementation : function\n        The original implementation passed in.\n    \"\"\")\n\n\n# exposed for testing purposes; used internally by _ArrayFunctionDispatcher\nadd_docstring(\n    _get_implementing_args,\n    \"\"\"\n    Collect arguments on which to call __array_function__.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    relevant_args : iterable of array-like\n        Iterable of possibly array-like arguments to check for\n        __array_function__ methods.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Sequence of arguments with __array_function__ methods, in the order in\n    which they should be called.\n    \"\"\")\n\n\nArgSpec = collections.namedtuple('ArgSpec', 'args varargs keywords defaults')\n\n\ndef verify_matching_signatures(implementation, dispatcher):\n    \"\"\"Verify that a dispatcher function has the right signature.\"\"\"\n    implementation_spec = ArgSpec(*getargspec(implementation))\n    dispatcher_spec = ArgSpec(*getargspec(dispatcher))\n\n    if (implementation_spec.args != dispatcher_spec.args or\n            implementation_spec.varargs != dispatcher_spec.varargs or\n            implementation_spec.keywords != dispatcher_spec.keywords or\n            (bool(implementation_spec.defaults) !=\n             bool(dispatcher_spec.defaults)) or\n            (implementation_spec.defaults is not None and\n             len(implementation_spec.defaults) !=\n             len(dispatcher_spec.defaults))):\n        raise RuntimeError('implementation and dispatcher for %s have '\n                           'different function signatures' % implementation)\n\n    if implementation_spec.defaults is not None:\n        if dispatcher_spec.defaults != (None,) * len(dispatcher_spec.defaults):\n            raise RuntimeError('dispatcher functions can only use None for '\n                               'default argument values')\n\n\ndef dispatch_array_function(dispatcher=None, module=None, verify=True,\n                            docs_from_dispatcher=False):\n    \"\"\"Decorator for adding dispatch with the __array_function__ protocol.\n\n    See NEP-18 for example usage.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    dispatcher : callable or None\n        Function that when called like ``dispatcher(*args, **kwargs)`` with\n        arguments from the NumPy function call returns an iterable of\n        array-like arguments to check for ``__array_function__``.\n\n        If `None`, the first argument is used as the single `like=` argument\n        and not passed on.  A function implementing `like=` must call its\n        dispatcher with `like` as the first non-keyword argument.\n    module : str, optional\n        __module__ attribute to set on new function, e.g., ``module='numpy'``.\n        By default, module is copied from the decorated function.\n    verify : bool, optional\n        If True, verify the that the signature of the dispatcher and decorated\n        function signatures match exactly: all required and optional arguments\n        should appear in order with the same names, but the default values for\n        all optional arguments should be ``None``. Only disable verification\n        if the dispatcher's signature needs to deviate for some particular\n        reason, e.g., because the function has a signature like\n        ``func(*args, **kwargs)``.\n    docs_from_dispatcher : bool, optional\n        If True, copy docs from the dispatcher function onto the dispatched\n        function, rather than from the implementation. This is useful for\n        functions defined in C, which otherwise don't have docstrings.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Function suitable for decorating the implementation of a NumPy function.\n\n    \"\"\"\n    def decorator(implementation):\n        if verify:\n            if dispatcher is not None:\n                verify_matching_signatures(implementation, dispatcher)\n            else:\n                # Using __code__ directly similar to verify_matching_signature\n                co = implementation.__code__\n                last_arg = co.co_argcount + co.co_kwonlyargcount - 1\n                last_arg = co.co_varnames[last_arg]\n                if last_arg != \"like\" or co.co_kwonlyargcount == 0:\n                    raise RuntimeError(\n                        \"__array_function__ expects `like=` to be the last \"\n                        \"argument and a keyword-only argument. \"\n                        f\"{implementation} does not seem to comply.\")\n\n        if docs_from_dispatcher:\n            add_docstring(implementation, dispatcher.__doc__)\n\n        public_api = _ArrayFunctionDispatcher(dispatcher, implementation)\n        public_api = functools.wraps(implementation)(public_api)\n\n        if module is not None:\n            public_api.__module__ = module\n\n        ARRAY_FUNCTIONS.add(public_api)\n\n        return public_api\n\n    return decorator\n\n\n# Backwards compatibility alias\narray_function_dispatch = dispatch_array_function\n\n\ndef array_function_from_dispatcher(\n        implementation, module=None, verify=True, docs_from_dispatcher=True):\n    \"\"\"Like array_function_dispatcher, but with function arguments flipped.\"\"\"\n\n    def decorator(dispatcher):\n        return dispatch_array_function(\n            dispatcher, module, verify=verify,\n            docs_from_dispatcher=docs_from_dispatcher)(implementation)\n    return decorator\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/numpy/_core/overrides.py"
}
Output
{
  "content": "\"\"\"Implementation of __array_function__ overrides from NEP-18.\"\"\"\nimport collections\nimport functools\n\nfrom .._utils import set_module\nfrom .._utils._inspect import getargspec\nfrom numpy._core._multiarray_umath import (\n    add_docstring,  _get_implementing_args, _ArrayFunctionDispatcher)\n\n\nARRAY_FUNCTIONS = set()\n\narray_function_like_doc = (\n    \"\"\"like : array_like, optional\n        Reference object to allow the creation of arrays which are not\n        NumPy arrays. If an array-like passed in as ``like`` supports\n        the ``__array_function__`` protocol, the result will be defined\n        by it. In this case, it ensures the creation of an array object\n        compatible with that passed in via this argument.\"\"\"\n)\n\ndef get_array_function_like_doc(public_api, docstring_template=\"\"):\n    ARRAY_FUNCTIONS.add(public_api)\n    docstring = public_api.__doc__ or docstring_template\n    return docstring.replace(\"${ARRAY_FUNCTION_LIKE}\", array_function_like_doc)\n\ndef finalize_array_function_like(public_api):\n    public_api.__doc__ = get_array_function_like_doc(public_api)\n    return public_api\n\n\nadd_docstring(\n    _ArrayFunctionDispatcher,\n    \"\"\"\n    Class to wrap functions with checks for __array_function__ overrides.\n\n    All arguments are required, and can only be passed by position.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    dispatcher : function or None\n        The dispatcher function that returns a single sequence-like object\n        of all arguments relevant.  It must have the same signature (except\n        the default values) as the actual implementation.\n        If ``None``, this is a ``like=`` dispatcher and the\n        ``_ArrayFunctionDispatcher`` must be called with ``like`` as the\n        first (additional and positional) argument.\n    implementation : function\n        Function that implements the operation on NumPy arrays without\n        overrides.  Arguments passed calling the ``_ArrayFunctionDispatcher``\n        will be forwarded to this (and the ``dispatcher``) as if using\n        ``*args, **kwargs``.\n\n    Attributes\n    ----------\n    _implementation : function\n        The original implementation passed in.\n    \"\"\")\n\n\n# exposed for testing purposes; used internally by _ArrayFunctionDispatcher\nadd_docstring(\n    _get_implementing_args,\n    \"\"\"\n    Collect arguments on which to call __array_function__.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    relevant_args : iterable of array-like\n        Iterable of possibly array-like arguments to check for\n        __array_function__ methods.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Sequence of arguments with __array_function__ methods, in the order in\n    which they should be called.\n    \"\"\")\n\n\nArgSpec = collections.namedtuple('ArgSpec', 'args varargs keywords defaults')\n\n\ndef verify_matching_signatures(implementation, dispatcher):\n    \"\"\"Verify that a dispatcher function has the right signature.\"\"\"\n    implementation_spec = ArgSpec(*getargspec(implementation))\n    dispatcher_spec = ArgSpec(*getargspec(dispatcher))\n\n    if (implementation_spec.args != dispatcher_spec.args or\n            implementation_spec.varargs != dispatcher_spec.varargs or\n            implementation_spec.keywords != dispatcher_spec.keywords or\n            (bool(implementation_spec.defaults) !=\n             bool(dispatcher_spec.defaults)) or\n            (implementation_spec.defaults is not None and\n             len(implementation_spec.defaults) !=\n             len(dispatcher_spec.defaults))):\n        raise RuntimeError('implementation and dispatcher for %s have '\n                           'different function signatures' % implementation)\n\n    if implementation_spec.defaults is not None:\n        if dispatcher_spec.defaults != (None,) * len(dispatcher_spec.defaults):\n            raise RuntimeError('dispatcher functions can only use None for '\n                               'default argument values')\n\n\ndef dispatch_array_function(dispatcher=None, module=None, verify=True,\n                            docs_from_dispatcher=False):\n    \"\"\"Decorator for adding dispatch with the __array_function__ protocol.\n\n    See NEP-18 for example usage.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    dispatcher : callable or None\n        Function that when called like ``dispatcher(*args, **kwargs)`` with\n        arguments from the NumPy function call returns an iterable of\n        array-like arguments to check for ``__array_function__``.\n\n        If `None`, the first argument is used as the single `like=` argument\n        and not passed on.  A function implementing `like=` must call its\n        dispatcher with `like` as the first non-keyword argument.\n    module : str, optional\n        __module__ attribute to set on new function, e.g., ``module='numpy'``.\n        By default, module is copied from the decorated function.\n    verify : bool, optional\n        If True, verify the that the signature of the dispatcher and decorated\n        function signatures match exactly: all required and optional arguments\n        should appear in order with the same names, but the default values for\n        all optional arguments should be ``None``. Only disable verification\n        if the dispatcher's signature needs to deviate for some particular\n        reason, e.g., because the function has a signature like\n        ``func(*args, **kwargs)``.\n    docs_from_dispatcher : bool, optional\n        If True, copy docs from the dispatcher function onto the dispatched\n        function, rather than from the implementation. This is useful for\n        functions defined in C, which otherwise don't have docstrings.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Function suitable for decorating the implementation of a NumPy function.\n\n    \"\"\"\n    def decorator(implementation):\n        if verify:\n            if dispatcher is not None:\n                verify_matching_signatures(implementation, dispatcher)\n            else:\n                # Using __code__ directly similar to verify_matching_signature\n                co = implementation.__code__\n                last_arg = co.co_argcount + co.co_kwonlyargcount - 1\n                last_arg = co.co_varnames[last_arg]\n                if last_arg != \"like\" or co.co_kwonlyargcount == 0:\n                    raise RuntimeError(\n                        \"__array_function__ expects `like=` to be the last \"\n                        \"argument and a keyword-only argument. \"\n                        f\"{implementation} does not seem to comply.\")\n\n        if docs_from_dispatcher:\n            add_docstring(implementation, dispatcher.__doc__)\n\n        public_api = _ArrayFunctionDispatcher(dispatcher, implementation)\n        public_api = functools.wraps(implementation)(public_api)\n\n        if module is not None:\n            public_api.__module__ = module\n\n        ARRAY_FUNCTIONS.add(public_api)\n\n        return public_api\n\n    return decorator\n\n\n# Backwards compatibility alias\narray_function_dispatch = dispatch_array_function\n\n\ndef array_function_from_dispatcher(\n        implementation, module=None, verify=True, docs_from_dispatcher=True):\n    \"\"\"Like array_function_dispatcher, but with function arguments flipped.\"\"\"\n\n    def decorator(dispatcher):\n        return dispatch_array_function(\n            dispatcher, module, verify=verify,\n            docs_from_dispatcher=docs_from_dispatcher)(implementation)\n    return decorator\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/numpy/_core/overrides.py",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}

_zeros_like_dispatcher(\\n59:     a, dtype=None, order=None, subok=None, shape=None, *, device=None\\n60: ):\\n61:     return (a,)\\n62: \\n63: \\n64: @array_function_dispatch(_zeros_like_dispatcher)\\n65: def zeros_like(\\n66:     a, dtype=None, order='K', subok=True, shape=None, *, device=None\\n67: ):\\n68:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n69:     Return an array of zeros with the same shape and type as a given array.\\n70: \\n71:     Parameters\\n72:     ----------\\n73:     a : array_like\\n74:         The shape and data-type of `a` define these same attributes of\\n75:         the returned array.\\n76:     dtype : data-type, optional\\n77:         Overrides the data type of the result.\\n78:     order : {'C', 'F', 'A', or 'K'}, optional\\n79:         Overrides the memory layout of the result. 'C' means C-order,\\n80:         'F' means F-order, 'A' means 'F' if `a` is Fortran contiguous,\\n81:         'C' otherwise. 'K' means match the layout of `a` as closely\\n82:         as possible.\\n83:     subok : bool, optional.\\n84:         If True, then the newly created array will use the sub-class\\n85:         type of `a`, otherwise it will be a base-class array. Defaults\\n86:         to True.\\n87:     shape : int or sequence of ints, optional.\\n88:         Overrides the shape of the result. If order='K' and the number of\\n89:         dimensions is unchanged, will try to keep order, otherwise,\\n90:         order='C' is implied.\\n91:     device : str, optional\\n92:         The device on which to place the created array. Default: None.\\n93:         For Array-API interoperability only, so must be ``\\\"cpu\\\"`` if passed.\\n94: \\n95:         .. versionadded:: 2.0.0\\n96: \\n97:     Returns\\n98:     -------\\n99:     out : ndarray\\n100:         Array of zeros with the same shape and type as `a`.\\n101: \\n102:     See Also\\n103:     --------\\n104:     empty_like : Return an empty array with shape and type of input.\\n105:     ones_like : Return an array of ones with shape and type of input.\\n106:     full_like : Return a new array with shape of input filled with value.\\n107:     zeros : Return a new array setting values to zero.\\n108: \\n109:     Examples\\n110:     --------\\n111:     >>> import numpy as np\\n112:     >>> x = np.arange(6)\\n113:     >>> x = x.reshape((2, 3))\\n114:     >>> x\\n115:     array([[0, 1, 2],\\n116:            [3, 4, 5]])\\n117:     >>> np.zeros_like(x)\\n118:     array([[0, 0, 0],\\n119:            [0, 0, 0]])\\n120: \\n121:     >>> y = np.arange(3, dtype=float)\\n122:     >>> y\\n123:     array([0., 1., 2.])\\n124:     >>> np.zeros_like(y)\\n125:     array([0.,  0.,  0.])\\n126: \\n127:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n128:     res = empty_like(\\n129:         a, dtype=dtype, order=order, subok=subok, shape=shape, device=device\\n130:     )\\n131:     # needed instead of a 0 to get same result as zeros for string dtypes\\n132:     z = zeros(1, dtype=res.dtype)\\n133:     multiarray.copyto(res, z, casting='unsafe')\\n134:     return res\\n135: \\n136: \\n137: @finalize_array_function_like\\n138: @set_module('numpy')\\n139: def ones(shape, dtype=None, order='C', *, device=None, like=None):\\n140:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n141:     Return a new array of given shape and type, filled with ones.\\n142: \\n143:     Parameters\\n144:     ----------\\n145:     shape : int or sequence of ints\\n146:         Shape of the new array, e.g., ``(2, 3)`` or ``2``.\\n147:     dtype : data-type, optional\\n148:         The desired data-type for the array, e.g., `numpy.int8`.  Default is\\n149:         `numpy.float64`.\\n150:     order : {'C', 'F'}, optional, default: C\\n151:         Whether to store multi-dimensional data in row-major\\n152:         (C-style) or column-major (Fortran-style) order in\\n153:         memory.\\n154:     device : str, optional\\n155:         The device on which to place the created array. Default: None.\\n156:         For Array-API interoperability only, so must be ``\\\"cpu\\\"`` if passed.\\n157: \\n158:         .. versionadded:: 2.0.0\\n159:     ${ARRAY_FUNCTION_LIKE}\\n160: \\n161:         .. versionadded:: 1.20.0\\n162: \\n163:     Returns\\n164:     -------\\n165:     out : ndarray\\n166:         Array of ones with the given shape, dtype, and order.\\n167: \\n168:     See Also\\n169:     --------\\n170:     ones_like : Return an array of ones with shape and type of input.\\n171:     empty : Return a new uninitialized array.\\n172:     zeros : Return a new array setting values to zero.\\n173:     full : Return a new array of given shape filled with value.\\n174: \\n175:     Examples\\n176:     --------\\n177:     >>> import numpy as np\\n178:     >>> np.ones(5)\\n179:     array([1., 1., 1., 1., 1.])\\n180: \\n181:     >>> np.ones((5,), dtype=int)\\n182:     array([1, 1, 1, 1, 1])\\n183: \\n184:     >>> np.ones((2, 1))\\n185:     array([[1.],\\n186:            [1.]])\\n187: \\n188:     >>> s = (2,2)\\n189:     >>> np.ones(s)\\n190:     array([[1.,  1.],\\n191:            [1.,  1.]])\\n192: \\n193:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n194:     if like is not None:\\n195:         return _ones_with_like(\\n196:             like, shape, dtype=dtype, order=order, device=device\\n197:         )\\n198: \\n199:     a = empty(shape, dtype, order, device=device)\\n200:     multiarray.copyto(a, 1, casting='unsafe')\\n201:     return a\\n202: \\n203: \\n204: _ones_with_like = array_function_dispatch()(ones)\\n205: \\n206: \\n207: def _ones_like_dispatcher(\\n208:     a, dtype=None, order=None, subok=None, shape=None, *, device=None\\n209: ):\\n210:     return (a,)\\n211: \\n212: \\n213: @array_function_dispatch(_ones_like_dispatcher)\\n214: def ones_like(\\n215:     a, dtype=None, order='K', subok=True, shape=None, *, device=None\\n216: ):\\n217:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n218:     Return an array of ones with the same shape and type as a given array.\\n219: \\n220:     Parameters\\n221:     ----------\\n222:     a : array_like\\n223:         The shape and data-type of `a` define these same attributes of\\n224:         the returned array.\\n225:     dtype : data-type, optional\\n226:         Overrides the data type of the result.\\n227:     order : {'C', 'F', 'A', or 'K'}, optional\\n228:         Overrides the memory layout of the result. 'C' means C-order,\\n229:         'F' means F-order, 'A' means 'F' if `a` is Fortran contiguous,\\n230:         'C' otherwise. 'K' means match the layout of `a` as closely\\n231:         as possible.\\n232:     subok : bool, optional.\\n233:         If True, then the newly created array will use the sub-class\\n234:         type of `a`, otherwise it will be a base-class array. Defaults\\n235:         to True.\\n236:     shape : int or sequence of ints, optional.\\n237:         Overrides the shape of the result. If order='K' and the number of\\n238:         dimensions is unchanged, will try to keep order, otherwise,\\n239:         order='C' is implied.\\n240:     device : str, optional\\n241:         The device on which to place the created array. Default: None.\\n242:         For Array-API interoperability only, so must be ``\\\"cpu\\\"`` if passed.\\n243: \\n244:         .. versionadded:: 2.0.0\\n245: \\n246:     Returns\\n247:     -------\\n248:     out : ndarray\\n249:         Array of ones with the same shape and type as `a`.\\n250: \\n251: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/numpy--v2.2.2@9ca88bff35bf60de74f9f95e6f90e6b4e0913e78/-/blob/numpy/_core/numeric.py%3FL1-250\"}",

from . import multiarray\n12: from . import numerictypes as nt\n13: from .multiarray import (\n14:     ALLOW_THREADS, BUFSIZE, CLIP, MAXDIMS, MAY_SHARE_BOUNDS, MAY_SHARE_EXACT,\n15:     RAISE, WRAP, arange, array, asarray, asanyarray, ascontiguousarray,\n16:     asfortranarray, broadcast, can_cast, concatenate, copyto, dot, dtype,\n17:     empty, empty_like, flatiter, frombuffer, from_dlpack, fromfile, fromiter,\n18:     fromstring, inner, lexsort, matmul, may_share_memory, min_scalar_type,\n19:     ndarray, nditer, nested_iters, promote_types, putmask, result_type,\n20:     shares_memory, finalize_array_function_like, set_module\n27: from .umath import (multiply, invert, sin, PINF, NAN)\n28: from . import numerictypes\n29: from ..exceptions import AxisError\n30: from ._ufunc_config module='numpy')\n38: \n39: \n40: __all__ = [\n41:     'newaxis', 'ndarray', 'flatiter', 'nditer', 'nested_iters', 'ufunc',\n42:     'arange', 'array', 'asarray', 'asanyarray', 'ascontiguousarray',\n43:     'asfortranarray', 'zeros', 'count_nonzero', 'empty', 'broadcast', 'dtype',\n44:     'fromstring', 'fromfile', 'frombuffer', 'from_dlpack', 'where',\n45:     'argwhere', 'copyto', 'concatenate', 'lexsort', 'astype',\n46:     'can_cast', 'promote_types', 'min_scalar_type',\n47:     'result_type', 'isfortran', 'empty_like', 'zeros_like', 'ones_like',\n48:     'correlate', 'convolve', 'inner', 'dot', 'outer', 'vdot', 'roll',\n49:     'rollaxis', 'moveaxis', 'cross', 'tensordot', 'little_endian',\n50:     'fromiter', 'array_equal', 'array_equiv', 'indices', 'fromfunction',\n51:     'isclose', 'isscalar', 'binary_repr', 'base_repr', 'ones',\n52:     'identity', 'allclose', 'putmask',\n53:     'flatnonzero', 'inf', 'nan', 'False_', 'True_', 'bitwise_not',\n54:     'full', 'full_like', 'matmul', 'vecdot', 'shares_memory',\n55:     'may_share_memory']\n56: \n57: \n58: def _zeros_like_dispatcher(\n59:     a, dtype=None, order=None, subok=None, shape=None, *, device=None\n60: ):\n61:     return (a,)\n62: \n63: \n64: @array_function_dispatch(_zeros_like_dispatcher)\n65: def zeros_like(\n66:     a, dtype=None, order='K', subok=True, shape=None, *, device=None\n67: ):\n68:     \"\"\"\n69:     Return an array of zeros with the same shape and type as a given array.\n70: \n71:     Parameters\n72:     ----------\n73:     a : array_like\n74:         The shape and data-type of `a` define these same attributes of\n75:         the returned array.\n76:     dtype : data-type, optional\n77:         Overrides the data type of the result.\n78:     order : {'C', 'F', 'A', or 'K'}, optional\n79:         Overrides the memory layout of the result. 'C' means C-order,\n80:         'F' means F-order, 'A' means 'F' if `a` is Fortran contiguous,\n81:         'C' otherwise. 'K' means match the layout of `a` as closely\n82:         as possible.\n83:     subok : bool, optional.\n84:         If True, then the newly created array will use the sub-class\n85:         type of `a`, otherwise it will be a base-class array. Defaults\n86:         to True.\n87:     shape : int or sequence of ints, optional.\n88:         Overrides the shape of the result. If order='K' and the number of\n89:         dimensions is unchanged, will try to keep order, otherwise,\n90:         order='C' is implied.\n91:     device : str, optional\n92:         The device on which to place the created array. Default: None.\n93:         For Array-API interoperability only, so must be ``\"cpu\"`` if passed.\n94: \n95:         .. versionadded:: 2.0.0\n96: \n97:     Returns\n98:     -------\n99:     out : ndarray\n100:         Array of zeros with the same shape and type as `a`.\n101: \n102:     See Also\n103:     --------\n104:     empty_like : Return an empty array with shape and type of input.\n105:     ones_like : Return an array of ones with shape and type of input.\n106:     full_like : Return a new array with shape of input filled with value.\n107:     zeros : Return a new array setting values to zero.\n108: \n109:     Examples\n110:     --------\n111:     >>> import numpy as np\n112:     >>> x = np.arange(6)\n113:     >>> x = x.reshape((2, 3))\n114:     >>> x\n115:     array([[0, 1, 2],\n116:            [3, 4, 5]])\n117:     >>> np.zeros_like(x)\n118:     array([[0, 0, 0],\n119:            [0, 0, 0]])\n120: \n121:     >>> y = np.arange(3, dtype=float)\n122:     >>> y\n123:     array([0., 1., 2.])\n124:     >>> np.zeros_like(y)\n125:     array([0.,  0.,  0.])\n126: \n127:     \"\"\"\n128:     res = empty_like(\n129:         a, dtype=dtype, order=order, subok=subok, shape=shape, device=device\n130:     )\n131:     # needed instead of a 0 to get same result as zeros for string dtypes\n132:     z = zeros(1, dtype=res.dtype)\n133:     multiarray.copyto(res, z, casting='unsafe')\n134:     return res\n135: \n136: \n137: @finalize_array_function_like\n138: @set_module('numpy')\n139: def ones(shape, dtype=None, order='C', *, device=None, like=None):\n140:     \"\"\"\n141:     Return a new array of given shape and type, filled with ones.\n142: \n143:     Parameters\n144:     ----------\n145:     shape : int or sequence of ints\n146:         Shape of the new array, e.g., ``(2, 3)`` or ``2``.\n147:     dtype : data-type, optional\n148:         The desired data-type for the array, e.g., `numpy.int8`.  Default is\n149:         `numpy.float64`.\n150:     order : {'C', 'F'}, optional, default: C\n151:         Whether to store multi-dimensional data in row-major\n152:         (C-style) or column-major (Fortran-style) order in\n153:         memory.\n154:     device : str, optional\n155:         The device on which to place the created array. Default: None.\n156:         For Array-API interoperability only, so must be ``\"cpu\"`` if passed.\n157: \n158:         .. versionadded:: 2.0.0\n159:     ${ARRAY_FUNCTION_LIKE}\n160: \n161:         .. versionadded:: 1.20.0\n162: \n163:     Returns\n164:     -------\n165:     out : ndarray\n166:         Array of ones with the given shape, dtype, and order.\n167: \n168:     See Also\n169:     --------\n170:     ones_like : Return an array of ones with shape and type of input.\n171:     empty : Return a new uninitialized array.\n172:     zeros : Return a new array setting values to zero.\n173:     full : Return a new array of given shape filled with value.\n174: \n175:     Examples\n176:     --------\n177:     >>> import numpy as np\n178:     >>> np.ones(5)\n179:     array([1., 1., 1., 1., 1.])\n180: \n181:     >>> np.ones((5,), dtype=int)\n182:     array([1, 1, 1, 1, 1])\n183: \n184:     >>> np.ones((2, 1))\n185:     array([[1.],\n186:            [1.]])\n187: \n188:     >>> s = (2,2)\n189:     >>> np.ones(s)\n190:     array([[1.,  1.],\n191:            [1.,  1.]])\n192: \n193:     \"\"\"\n194:     if like is not None:\n195:         return _ones_with_like(\n196:             like, shape, dtype=dtype, order=order, device=device\n197:         )\n198: \n199:     a = empty(shape, dtype, order, device=device)\n200:     multiarray.copyto(a, 1, casting='unsafe')\n201:     return a\n202: \n203: \n204: _ones_with_like = array_function_dispatch()(ones)\n205: \n206: \n207: def _ones_like_dispatcher(\n208:     a, dtype=None, order=None, subok=None, shape=None, *, device=None\n209: ):\n210:     return (a,)\n211: \n212: \n213: @array_function_dispatch(_ones_like_dispatcher)\n214: def ones_like(\n215:     a, dtype=None, order='K', subok=True, shape=None, *, device=None\n216: ):\n217:     \"\"\"\n218:     Return an array of ones with the same shape and type as a given array.\n219: \n220:     Parameters\n221:     ----------\n222:     a : array_like\n223:         The shape and data-type of `a` define these same attributes of\n224:         the returned array.\n225:     dtype : data-type, optional\n226:         Overrides the data type of the result.\n227:     order : {'C', 'F', 'A', or 'K'}, optional\n228:         Overrides the memory layout of the result. 'C' means C-order,\n229:         'F' means F-order, 'A' means 'F' if `a` is Fortran contiguous,\n230:         'C' otherwise. 'K' means match the layout of `a` as closely\n231:         as possible.\n232:     subok : bool, optional.\n233:         If True, then the newly created array will use the sub-class\n234:         type of `a`, otherwise it will be a base-class array. Defaults\n235:         to True.\n236:     shape : int or sequence of ints, optional.\n237:         Overrides the shape of the result. If order='K' and the number of\n238:         dimensions is unchanged, will try to keep order, otherwise,\n239:         order='C' is implied.\n240:     device : str, optional\n241:         The device on which to place the created array. Default: None.\n242:         For Array-API interoperability only, so must be ``\"cpu\"`` if passed.\n243: \n244:         .. versionadded:: 2.0.0\n245: \n246:     Returns\n247:     -------\n248:     out : ndarray\n249:         Array of ones with the same shape and type as `a`.\n250: \n251: ",

{
  "filePath": "/workspace/numpy/_core/overrides.py",
  "newString": "def array_function_from_dispatcher(",
  "oldString": "# Backwards compatibility alias\narray_function_dispatch = dispatch_array_function\n\n\ndef array_function_from_dispatcher(",
  "originalFile": "\"\"\"Implementation of __array_function__ overrides from NEP-18.\"\"\"\nimport collections\nimport functools\n\nfrom .._utils import set_module\nfrom .._utils._inspect import getargspec\nfrom numpy._core._multiarray_umath import (\n    add_docstring,  _get_implementing_args, _ArrayFunctionDispatcher)\n\n\nARRAY_FUNCTIONS = set()\n\narray_function_like_doc = (\n    \"\"\"like : array_like, optional\n        Reference object to allow the creation of arrays which are not\n        NumPy arrays. If an array-like passed in as ``like`` supports\n        the ``__array_function__`` protocol, the result will be defined\n        by it. In this case, it ensures the creation of an array object\n        compatible with that passed in via this argument.\"\"\"\n)\n\ndef get_array_function_like_doc(public_api, docstring_template=\"\"):\n    ARRAY_FUNCTIONS.add(public_api)\n    docstring = public_api.__doc__ or docstring_template\n    return docstring.replace(\"${ARRAY_FUNCTION_LIKE}\", array_function_like_doc)\n\ndef finalize_array_function_like(public_api):\n    public_api.__doc__ = get_array_function_like_doc(public_api)\n    return public_api\n\n\nadd_docstring(\n    _ArrayFunctionDispatcher,\n    \"\"\"\n    Class to wrap functions with checks for __array_function__ overrides.\n\n    All arguments are required, and can only be passed by position.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    dispatcher : function or None\n        The dispatcher function that returns a single sequence-like object\n        of all arguments relevant.  It must have the same signature (except\n        the default values) as the actual implementation.\n        If ``None``, this is a ``like=`` dispatcher and the\n        ``_ArrayFunctionDispatcher`` must be called with ``like`` as the\n        first (additional and positional) argument.\n    implementation : function\n        Function that implements the operation on NumPy arrays without\n        overrides.  Arguments passed calling the ``_ArrayFunctionDispatcher``\n        will be forwarded to this (and the ``dispatcher``) as if using\n        ``*args, **kwargs``.\n\n    Attributes\n    ----------\n    _implementation : function\n        The original implementation passed in.\n    \"\"\")\n\n\n# exposed for testing purposes; used internally by _ArrayFunctionDispatcher\nadd_docstring(\n    _get_implementing_args,\n    \"\"\"\n    Collect arguments on which to call __array_function__.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    relevant_args : iterable of array-like\n        Iterable of possibly array-like arguments to check for\n        __array_function__ methods.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Sequence of arguments with __array_function__ methods, in the order in\n    which they should be called.\n    \"\"\")\n\n\nArgSpec = collections.namedtuple('ArgSpec', 'args varargs keywords defaults')\n\n\ndef verify_matching_signatures(implementation, dispatcher):\n    \"\"\"Verify that a dispatcher function has the right signature.\"\"\"\n    implementation_spec = ArgSpec(*getargspec(implementation))\n    dispatcher_spec = ArgSpec(*getargspec(dispatcher))\n\n    if (implementation_spec.args != dispatcher_spec.args or\n            implementation_spec.varargs != dispatcher_spec.varargs or\n            implementation_spec.keywords != dispatcher_spec.keywords or\n            (bool(implementation_spec.defaults) !=\n             bool(dispatcher_spec.defaults)) or\n            (implementation_spec.defaults is not None and\n             len(implementation_spec.defaults) !=\n             len(dispatcher_spec.defaults))):\n        raise RuntimeError('implementation and dispatcher for %s have '\n                           'different function signatures' % implementation)\n\n    if implementation_spec.defaults is not None:\n        if dispatcher_spec.defaults != (None,) * len(dispatcher_spec.defaults):\n            raise RuntimeError('dispatcher functions can only use None for '\n                               'default argument values')\n\n\ndef dispatch_array_function(dispatcher=None, module=None, verify=True,\n                            docs_from_dispatcher=False):\n    \"\"\"Decorator for adding dispatch with the __array_function__ protocol.\n\n    See NEP-18 for example usage.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    dispatcher : callable or None\n        Function that when called like ``dispatcher(*args, **kwargs)`` with\n        arguments from the NumPy function call returns an iterable of\n        array-like arguments to check for ``__array_function__``.\n\n        If `None`, the first argument is used as the single `like=` argument\n        and not passed on.  A function implementing `like=` must call its\n        dispatcher with `like` as the first non-keyword argument.\n    module : str, optional\n        __module__ attribute to set on new function, e.g., ``module='numpy'``.\n        By default, module is copied from the decorated function.\n    verify : bool, optional\n        If True, verify the that the signature of the dispatcher and decorated\n        function signatures match exactly: all required and optional arguments\n        should appear in order with the same names, but the default values for\n        all optional arguments should be ``None``. Only disable verification\n        if the dispatcher's signature needs to deviate for some particular\n        reason, e.g., because the function has a signature like\n        ``func(*args, **kwargs)``.\n    docs_from_dispatcher : bool, optional\n        If True, copy docs from the dispatcher function onto the dispatched\n        function, rather than from the implementation. This is useful for\n        functions defined in C, which otherwise don't have docstrings.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Function suitable for decorating the implementation of a NumPy function.\n\n    \"\"\"\n    def decorator(implementation):\n        if verify:\n            if dispatcher is not None:\n                verify_matching_signatures(implementation, dispatcher)\n            else:\n                # Using __code__ directly similar to verify_matching_signature\n                co = implementation.__code__\n                last_arg = co.co_argcount + co.co_kwonlyargcount - 1\n                last_arg = co.co_varnames[last_arg]\n                if last_arg != \"like\" or co.co_kwonlyargcount == 0:\n                    raise RuntimeError(\n                        \"__array_function__ expects `like=` to be the last \"\n                        \"argument and a keyword-only argument. \"\n                        f\"{implementation} does not seem to comply.\")\n\n        if docs_from_dispatcher:\n            add_docstring(implementation, dispatcher.__doc__)\n\n        public_api = _ArrayFunctionDispatcher(dispatcher, implementation)\n        public_api = functools.wraps(implementation)(public_api)\n\n        if module is not None:\n            public_api.__module__ = module\n\n        ARRAY_FUNCTIONS.add(public_api)\n\n        return public_api\n\n    return decorator\n\n\n# Backwards compatibility alias\narray_function_dispatch = dispatch_array_function\n\n\ndef array_function_from_dispatcher(\n        implementation, module=None, verify=True, docs_from_dispatcher=True):\n    \"\"\"Like array_function_dispatcher, but with function arguments flipped.\"\"\"\n\n    def decorator(dispatcher):\n        return dispatch_array_function(\n            dispatcher, module, verify=verify,\n            docs_from_dispatcher=docs_from_dispatcher)(implementation)\n    return decorator\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "     return decorator",
        " ",
        " ",
        "-# Backwards compatibility alias",
        "-array_function_dispatch = dispatch_array_function",
        "-",
        "-",
        " def array_function_from_dispatcher(",
        "         implementation, module=None, verify=True, docs_from_dispatcher=True):",
        "     \"\"\"Like array_function_dispatcher, but with function arguments flipped.\"\"\""
      ],
      "newLines": 6,
      "newStart": 170,
      "oldLines": 10,
      "oldStart": 170
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

<https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/12099>`__: ENH: Validate dispatcher functions in array_function_dispatch\\n436: * `#12102 <https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/12102>`__: TST: improve coverage of nd_grid\\n437: \",\"endLine\":437,\"startLine\":434},{\"content\":\"507: * `#12268 <https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/12268>`__: DOC: add broadcasting article from scipy old-wiki\\n508: * `#12270 <https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/12270>`__: MAINT: set `__module__` for more `array_function_dispatch` uses\\n509: * `#12276 <https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/12276>`__: MAINT: remove unused parse_index()\\n510: \",\"endLine\":510,\"startLine\":507}],\"file\":\"doc/changelog/1.16.0-changelog.rst\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/numpy--v2.2.2\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/numpy--v2.2.2/-/blob/doc/changelog/1.16.0-changelog.rst\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"544: * `#13523 <https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/13523>`__: BUG: distutils/system_info.py fix missing subprocess import\\n545: * `#13529 <https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/13529>`__: MAINT: Use exec() instead array_function_dispatch to improve...\\n546: * `#13530 <https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/13530>`__: BENCH: Modify benchmarks for radix sort.\\n547: \",\"endLine\":547,\"startLine\":544}],\"file\":\"doc/changelog/1.17.0-changelog.rst\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/numpy--v2.2.2\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/numpy--v2.2.2/-/blob/doc/changelog/1.17.0-changelog.rst\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"354: The private ``_implementation`` attribute, defined below in the\\n355: ``array_function_dispatch`` decorator, allows us to avoid the special cases for\\n356: NumPy arrays that were needed in the ``__array_ufunc__`` protocol.\\n357: \",\"endLine\":357,\"startLine\":354},{\"content\":\"385: \\n386: To achieve this, we define a ``array_function_dispatch`` decorator to rewrite\\n387: NumPy functions. The basic implementation is as follows:\\n388: \",\"endLine\":388,\"startLine\":385},{\"content\":\"390: \\n391:     def array_function_dispatch(dispatcher, module=None):\\n392:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Wrap a function for dispatch with the __array_function__ protocol.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n393: \",\"endLine\":393,\"startLine\":390}],\"file\":\"doc/neps/nep-0018-array-function-protocol.rst\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/numpy--v2.2.2\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/numpy--v2.2.2/-/blob/doc/neps/nep-0018-array-function-protocol.rst\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"279: To permit ``__array_function__`` dispatching, one possible implementation is to\\n280: decorate Python functions with ``overrides.array_function_dispatch``, but C\\n281: functions have a different requirement, which we shall describe shortly.\\n282: \",\"endLine\":282,\"startLine\":279},{\"content\":\"283: The example below shows a suggestion on how the ``asarray`` could be decorated\\n284: with ``overrides.array_function_dispatch``:\\n285: \\n286: \",\"endLine\":286,\"startLine\":283},{\"content\":\"291:     @set_module('numpy')\\n292:     @array_function_dispatch(_asarray_decorator)\\n293:     def asarray(a, dtype=None, order=None, *, like=None):\\n294: \",\"endLine\":294,\"startLine\":291}],\"file\":\"doc/neps/nep-0035-array-creation-dispatch-with-array-function.rst\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/numpy--v2.2.2\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/numpy--v2.2.2/-/blob/doc/neps/nep-0035-array-creation-dispatch-with-array-function.rst\"}]}"

Output
"{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"Implementation of __array_function__ overrides from NEP-18.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: import collections\\n3: import functools\\n4: \\n5: from .._utils import set_module\\n6: from .._utils._inspect import getargspec\\n7: from numpy._core._multiarray_umath import (\\n8:     add_docstring,  _get_implementing_args, _ArrayFunctionDispatcher)\\n9: \\n10: \\n11: ARRAY_FUNCTIONS = set()\\n12: \\n13: array_function_like_doc = (\\n14:     \\\"\\\"\\\"like : array_like, optional\\n15:         Reference object to allow the creation of arrays which are not\\n16:         NumPy arrays. If an array-like passed in as ``like`` supports\\n17:         the ``__array_function__`` protocol, the result will be defined\\n18:         by it. In this case, it ensures the creation of an array object\\n19:         compatible with that passed in via this argument.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n20: )\\n21: \\n22: def get_array_function_like_doc(public_api, docstring_template=\\\"\\\"):\\n23:     ARRAY_FUNCTIONS.add(public_api)\\n24:     docstring = public_api.__doc__ or docstring_template\\n25:     return docstring.replace(\\\"${ARRAY_FUNCTION_LIKE}\\\", array_function_like_doc)\\n26: \\n27: def finalize_array_function_like(public_api):\\n28:     public_api.__doc__ = get_array_function_like_doc(public_api)\\n29:     return public_api\\n30: \\n31: \\n32: add_docstring(\\n33:     _ArrayFunctionDispatcher,\\n34:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n35:     Class to wrap functions with checks for __array_function__ overrides.\\n36: \\n37:     All arguments are required, and can only be passed by position.\\n38: \\n39:     Parameters\\n40:     ----------\\n41:     dispatcher : function or None\\n42:         The dispatcher function that returns a single sequence-like object\\n43:         of all arguments relevant.  It must have the same signature (except\\n44:         the default values) as the actual implementation.\\n45:         If ``None``, this is a ``like=`` dispatcher and the\\n46:         ``_ArrayFunctionDispatcher`` must be called with ``like`` as the\\n47:         first (additional and positional) argument.\\n48:     implementation : function\\n49:         Function that implements the operation on NumPy arrays without\\n50:         overrides.  Arguments passed calling the ``_ArrayFunctionDispatcher``\\n51:         will be forwarded to this (and the ``dispatcher``) as if using\\n52:         ``*args, **kwargs``.\\n53: \\n54:     Attributes\\n55:     ----------\\n56:     _implementation : function\\n57:         The original implementation passed in.\\n58:     \\\"\\\"\\\")\\n59: \\n60: \\n61: # exposed for testing purposes; used internally by _ArrayFunctionDispatcher\\n62: add_docstring(\\n63:     _get_implementing_args,\\n64:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n65:     Collect arguments on which to call __array_function__.\\n66: \\n67:     Parameters\\n68:     ----------\\n69:     relevant_args : iterable of array-like\\n70:         Iterable of possibly array-like arguments to check for\\n71:         __array_function__ methods.\\n72: \\n73:     Returns\\n74:     -------\\n75:     Sequence of arguments with __array_function__ methods, in the order in\\n76:     which they should be called.\\n77:     \\\"\\\"\\\")\\n78: \\n79: \\n80: ArgSpec = collections.namedtuple('ArgSpec', 'args varargs keywords defaults')\\n81: \\n82: \\n83: def verify_matching_signatures(implementation, dispatcher):\\n84:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Verify that a dispatcher function has the right signature.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n85:     implementation_spec = ArgSpec(*getargspec(implementation))\\n86:     dispatcher_spec = ArgSpec(*getargspec(dispatcher))\\n87: \\n88:     if (implementation_spec.args != dispatcher_spec.args or\\n89:             implementation_spec.varargs != dispatcher_spec.varargs or\\n90:             implementation_spec.keywords != dispatcher_spec.keywords or\\n91:             (bool(implementation_spec.defaults) !=\\n92:              bool(dispatcher_spec.defaults)) or\\n93:             (implementation_spec.defaults is not None and\\n94:              len(implementation_spec.defaults) !=\\n95:              len(dispatcher_spec.defaults))):\\n96:         raise RuntimeError('implementation and dispatcher for %s have '\\n97:                            'different function signatures' % implementation)\\n98: \\n99:     if implementation_spec.defaults is not None:\\n100:         if dispatcher_spec.defaults != (None,) * len(dispatcher_spec.defaults):\\n101:             raise RuntimeError('dispatcher functions can only use None for '\\n102:                                'default argument values')\\n103: \\n104: \\n105: def array_function_dispatch(dispatcher=None, module=None, verify=True,\\n106:                             docs_from_dispatcher=False):\\n107:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Decorator for adding dispatch with the __array_function__ protocol.\\n108: \\n109:     See NEP-18 for example usage.\\n110: \\n111:     Parameters\\n112:     ----------\\n113:     dispatcher : callable or None\\n114:         Function that when called like ``dispatcher(*args, **kwargs)`` with\\n115:         arguments from the NumPy function call returns an iterable of\\n116:         array-like arguments to check for ``__array_function__``.\\n117: \\n118:         If `None`, the first argument is used as the single `like=` argument\\n119:         and not passed on.  A function implementing `like=` must call its\\n120:         dispatcher with `like` as the first non-keyword argument.\\n121:     module : str, optional\\n122:         __module__ attribute to set on new function, e.g., ``module='numpy'``.\\n123:         By default, module is copied from the decorated function.\\n124:     verify : bool, optional\\n125:         If True, verify the that the signature of the dispatcher and decorated\\n126:         function signatures match exactly: all required and optional arguments\\n127:         should appear in order with the same names, but the default values for\\n128:         all optional arguments should be ``None``. Only disable verification\\n129:         if the dispatcher's signature needs to deviate for some particular\\n130:         reason, e.g., because the function has a signature like\\n131:         ``func(*args, **kwargs)``.\\n132:     docs_from_dispatcher : bool, optional\\n133:         If True, copy docs from the dispatcher function onto the dispatched\\n134:         function, rather than from the implementation. This is useful for\\n135:         functions defined in C, which otherwise don't have docstrings.\\n136: \\n137:     Returns\\n138:     -------\\n139:     Function suitable for decorating the implementation of a NumPy function.\\n140: \\n141:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n142:     def decorator(implementation):\\n143:         if verify:\\n144:             if dispatcher is not None:\\n145:                 verify_matching_signatures(implementation, dispatcher)\\n146:             else:\\n147:                 # Using __code__ directly similar to verify_matching_signature\\n148:                 co = implementation.__code__\\n149:                 last_arg = co.co_argcount + co.co_kwonlyargcount - 1\\n150:                 last_arg = co.co_varnames[last_arg]\\n151:                 if last_arg != \\\"like\\\" or co.co_kwonlyargcount == 0:\\n152:                     raise RuntimeError(\\n153:                         \\\"__array_function__ expects `like=` to be the last \\\"\\n154:                         \\\"argument and a keyword-only argument. \\\"\\n155:                         f\\\"{implementation} does not seem to comply.\\\")\\n156: \\n157:         if docs_from_dispatcher:\\n158:             add_docstring(implementation, dispatcher.__doc__)\\n159: \\n160:         public_api = _ArrayFunctionDispatcher(dispatcher, implementation)\\n161:         public_api = functools.wraps(implementation)(public_api)\\n162: \\n163:         if module is not None:\\n164:             public_api.__module__ = module\\n165: \\n166:         ARRAY_FUNCTIONS.add(public_api)\\n167: \\n168:         return public_api\\n169: \\n170:     return decorator\\n171: \\n172: \\n173: def array_function_from_dispatcher(\\n174:         implementation, module=None, verify=True, docs_from_dispatcher=True):\\n175:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Like array_function_dispatcher, but with function arguments flipped.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n176: \\n177:     def decorator(dispatcher):\\n178:         return array_function_dispatch(\\n179:             dispatcher, module, verify=verify,\\n180:             docs_from_dispatcher=docs_from_dispatcher)(implementation)\\n181:     return decorator\\n182: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/numpy--v2.2.2@9ca88bff35bf60de74f9f95e6f90e6b4e0913e78/-/blob/numpy/_core/overrides.py%3FL1-200\"}"

"{\"content\":\"30: from ._ufunc_config import errstate\\n31: \\n32: bitwise_not = invert\\n33: ufunc = type(sin)\\n34: newaxis = None\\n35: \\n36: array_function_dispatch = functools.partial(\\n37:     overrides.array_function_dispatch, module='numpy')\\n38: \\n39: \\n40: __all__ = [\\n41:     'newaxis', 'ndarray', 'flatiter', 'nditer', 'nested_iters', 'ufunc',\\n42:     'arange', 'array', 'asarray', 'asanyarray', 'ascontiguousarray',\\n43:     'asfortranarray', 'zeros', 'count_nonzero', 'empty', 'broadcast', 'dtype',\\n44:     'fromstring', 'fromfile', 'frombuffer', 'from_dlpack', 'where',\\n45:     'argwhere', 'copyto', 'concatenate', 'lexsort', 'astype',\\n46:     'can_cast', 'promote_types', 'min_scalar_type',\\n47:     'result_type', 'isfortran', 'empty_like', 'zeros_like', 'ones_like',\\n48:     'correlate', 'convolve', 'inner', 'dot', 'outer', 'vdot', 'roll',\\n49:     'rollaxis', 'moveaxis', 'cross', 'tensordot', 'little_endian',\\n50:     'fromiter', 'array_equal', 'array_equiv', 'indices', 'fromfunction',\\n51:     'isclose', 'isscalar', 'binary_repr', 'base_repr', 'ones',\\n52:     'identity', 'allclose', 'putmask',\\n53:     'flatnonzero', 'inf', 'nan', 'False_', 'True_', 'bitwise_not',\\n54:     'full', 'full_like', 'matmul', 'vecdot', 'shares_memory',\\n55:     'may_share_memory']\\n56: \\n57: \\n58: def _zeros_like_dispatcher(\\n59:     a, dtype=None, order=None, subok=None, shape=None, *, device=None\\n60: ):\\n61:     return (a,)\\n62: \\n63: \\n64: @array_function_dispatch(_zeros_like_dispatcher)\\n65: def zeros_like(\\n66:     a, dtype=None, order='K', subok=True, shape=None, *, device=None\\n67: ):\\n68:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n69:     Return an array of zeros with the same shape and type as a given array.\\n70: \\n71:     Parameters\\n72:     ----------\\n73:     a : array_like\\n74:         The shape and data-type of `a` define these same attributes of\\n75:         the returned array.\\n76:     dtype : data-type, optional\\n77:         Overrides the data type of the result.\\n78:     order : {'C', 'F', 'A', or 'K'}, optional\\n79:         Overrides the memory layout of the result. 'C' means C-order,\\n80:         'F' means F-order, 'A' means 'F' if `a` is Fortran contiguous,\\n81:         'C' otherwise. 'K' means match the layout of `a` as closely\\n82:         as possible.\\n83:     subok : bool, optional.\\n84:         If True, then the newly created array will use the sub-class\\n85:         type of `a`, otherwise it will be a base-class array. Defaults\\n86:         to True.\\n87:     shape : int or sequence of ints, optional.\\n88:         Overrides the shape of the result. If order='K' and the number of\\n89:         dimensions is unchanged, will try to keep order, otherwise,\\n90:         order='C' is implied.\\n91:     device : str, optional\\n92:         The device on which to place the created array. Default: None.\\n93:         For Array-API interoperability only, so must be ``\\\"cpu\\\"`` if passed.\\n94: \\n95:         .. versionadded:: 2.0.0\\n96: \\n97:     Returns\\n98:     -------\\n99:     out : ndarray\\n100:         Array of zeros with the same shape and type as `a`.\\n101: \\n102:     See Also\\n103:     --------\\n104:     empty_like : Return an empty array with shape and type of input.\\n105:     ones_like : Return an array of ones with shape and type of input.\\n106:     full_like : Return a new array with shape of input filled with value.\\n107:     zeros : Return a new array setting values to zero.\\n108: \\n109:     Examples\\n110:     --------\\n111:     >>> import numpy as np\\n112:     >>> x = np.arange(6)\\n113:     >>> x = x.reshape((2, 3))\\n114:     >>> x\\n115:     array([[0, 1, 2],\\n116:            [3, 4, 5]])\\n117:     >>> np.zeros_like(x)\\n118:     array([[0, 0, 0],\\n119:            [0, 0, 0]])\\n120: \\n121:     >>> y = np.arange(3, dtype=float)\\n122:     >>> y\\n123:     array([0., 1., 2.])\\n124:     >>> np.zeros_like(y)\\n125:     array([0.,  0.,  0.])\\n126: \\n127:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n128:     res = empty_like(\\n129:         a, dtype=dtype, order=order, subok=subok, shape=shape, device=device\\n130:     )\\n131:     # needed instead of a 0 to get same result as zeros for string dtypes\\n132:     z = zeros(1, dtype=res.dtype)\\n133:     multiarray.copyto(res, z, casting='unsafe')\\n134:     return res\\n135: \\n136: \\n137: @finalize_array_function_like\\n138: @set_module('numpy')\\n139: def ones(shape, dtype=None, order='C', *, device=None, like=None):\\n140:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n141:     Return a new array of given shape and type, filled with ones.\\n142: \\n143:     Parameters\\n144:     ----------\\n145:     shape : int or sequence of ints\\n146:         Shape of the new array, e.g., ``(2, 3)`` or ``2``.\\n147:     dtype : data-type, optional\\n148:         The desired data-type for the array, e.g., `numpy.int8`.  Default is\\n149:         `numpy.float64`.\\n150:     order : {'C', 'F'}, optional, default: C\\n151:         Whether to store multi-dimensional data in row-major\\n152:         (C-style) or column-major (Fortran-style) order in\\n153:         memory.\\n154:     device : str, optional\\n155:         The device on which to place the created array. Default: None.\\n156:         For Array-API interoperability only, so must be ``\\\"cpu\\\"`` if passed.\\n157: \\n158:         .. versionadded:: 2.0.0\\n159:     ${ARRAY_FUNCTION_LIKE}\\n160: \\n161:         .. versionadded:: 1.20.0\\n162: \\n163:     Returns\\n164:     -------\\n165:     out : ndarray\\n166:         Array of ones with the given shape, dtype, and order.\\n167: \\n168:     See Also\\n169:     --------\\n170:     ones_like : Return an array of ones with shape and type of input.\\n171:     empty : Return a new uninitialized array.\\n172:     zeros : Return a new array setting values to zero.\\n173:     full : Return a new array of given shape filled with value.\\n174: \\n175:     Examples\\n176:     --------\\n177:     >>> import numpy as np\\n178:     >>> np.ones(5)\\n179:     array([1., 1., 1., 1., 1.])\\n180: \\n181:     >>> np.ones((5,), dtype=int)\\n182:     array([1, 1, 1, 1, 1])\\n183: \\n184:     >>> np.ones((2, 1))\\n185:     array([[1.],\\n186:            [1.]])\\n187: \\n188:     >>> s = (2,2)\\n189:     >>> np.ones(s)\\n190:     array([[1.,  1.],\\n191:            [1.,  1.]])\\n192: \\n193:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n194:     if like is not None:\\n195:         return _ones_with_like(\\n196:             like, shape, dtype=dtype, order=order, device=device\\n197:         )\\n198: \\n199:     a = empty(shape, dtype, order, device=device)\\n200:     multiarray.copyto(a, 1, casting='unsafe')\\n201:     return a\\n202: \\n203: \\n204: _ones_with_like = array_function_dispatch()(ones)\\n205: \\n206: \\n207: def _ones_like_dispatcher(\\n208:     a, dtype=None, order=None, subok=None, shape=None, *, device=None\\n209: ):\\n210:     return (a,)\\n211: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/numpy--v2.2.2@9ca88bff35bf60de74f9f95e6f90e6b4e0913e78/-/blob/numpy/_core/numeric.py%3FL30-210\"}"

module='numpy')\\n38: \\n39: \\n40: __all__ = [\\n41:     'newaxis', 'ndarray', 'flatiter', 'nditer', 'nested_iters', 'ufunc',\\n42:     'arange', 'array', 'asarray', 'asanyarray', 'indices', 'fromfunction',\\n51:     'isclose', 'isscalar', 'binary_repr', 'base_repr', 'ones',\\n52:     'identity', 'allclose', 'putmask',\\n53:     'flatnonzero', 'inf', 'nan', 'False_', 'True_', 'bitwise_not',\\n54:     'full', 'full_like', 'matmul', 'vecdot', 'shares_memory',\\n55:     'may_share_memory']\\n56: \\n57: \\n58: def _zeros_like_dispatcher(\\n59:     a, dtype=None, order=None, subok=None, shape=None, *, device=None\\n60: ):\\n61:     return (a,)\\n62: \\n63: \\n64: @array_function_dispatch(_zeros_like_dispatcher)\\n65: def zeros_like(\\n66:     a, dtype=None, order='K', subok=True, shape=None, *, device=None\\n67: ):\\n68:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n69:     Return an array of zeros with the same shape and type as a given array.\\n70: \\n71:     Parameters\\n72:     ----------\\n73:     a : array_like\\n74:         The shape and data-type of `a` define these same attributes of\\n75:         the returned array.\\n76:     dtype : data-type, optional\\n77:         Overrides the data type of the result.\\n78:     order : {'C', 'F', 'A', or 'K'}, optional\\n79:         Overrides the memory layout of the result. 'C' means C-order,\\n80:         'F' means F-order, 'A' means 'F' if `a` is Fortran contiguous,\\n81:         'C' otherwise. 'K' means match the layout of `a` as closely\\n82:         as possible.\\n83:     subok : bool, optional.\\n84:         If True, then the newly created array will use the sub-class\\n85:         type of `a`, otherwise it will be a base-class array. Defaults\\n86:         to True.\\n87:     shape : int or sequence of ints, optional.\\n88:         Overrides the shape of the result. If order='K' and the number of\\n89:         dimensions is unchanged, will try to keep order, otherwise,\\n90:         order='C' is implied.\\n91:     device : str, optional\\n92:         The device on which to place the created array. Default: None.\\n93:         For Array-API interoperability only, so must be ``\\\"cpu\\\"`` if passed.\\n94: \\n95:         .. versionadded:: 2.0.0\\n96: \\n97:     Returns\\n98:     -------\\n99:     out : ndarray\\n100:         Array of zeros with the same shape and type as `a`.\\n101: \\n102:     See Also\\n103:     --------\\n104:     empty_like : Return an empty array with shape and type of input.\\n105:     ones_like : Return an array of ones with shape and type of input.\\n106:     full_like : Return a new array with shape of input filled with value.\\n107:     zeros : Return a new array setting values to zero.\\n108: \\n109:     Examples\\n110:     --------\\n111:     >>> import numpy as np\\n112:     >>> x = np.arange(6)\\n113:     >>> x = x.reshape((2, 3))\\n114:     >>> x\\n115:     array([[0, 1, 2],\\n116:            [3, 4, 5]])\\n117:     >>> np.zeros_like(x)\\n118:     array([[0, 0, 0],\\n119:            [0, 0, 0]])\\n120: \\n121:     >>> y = np.arange(3, dtype=float)\\n122:     >>> y\\n123:     array([0., 1., 2.])\\n124:     >>> np.zeros_like(y)\\n125:     array([0.,  0.,  0.])\\n126: \\n127:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n128:     res = empty_like(\\n129:         a, dtype=dtype, order=order, subok=subok, shape=shape, device=device\\n130:     )\\n131:     # needed instead of a 0 to get same result as zeros for string dtypes\\n132:     z = zeros(1, dtype=res.dtype)\\n133:     multiarray.copyto(res, z, casting='unsafe')\\n134:     return res\\n135: \\n136: \\n137: @finalize_array_function_like\\n138: @set_module('numpy')\\n139: def ones(shape, dtype=None, order='C', *, device=None, like=None):\\n140:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n141:     Return a new array of given shape and type, filled with ones.\\n142: \\n143:     Parameters\\n144:     ----------\\n145:     shape : int or sequence of ints\\n146:         Shape of the new array, e.g., ``(2, 3)`` or ``2``.\\n147:     dtype : data-type, optional\\n148:         The desired data-type for the array, e.g., `numpy.int8`.  Default is\\n149:         `numpy.float64`.\\n150:     order : {'C', 'F'}, optional, default: C\\n151:         Whether to store multi-dimensional data in row-major\\n152:         (C-style) or column-major (Fortran-style) order in\\n153:         memory.\\n154:     device : str, optional\\n155:         The device on which to place the created array. Default: None.\\n156:         For Array-API interoperability only, so must be ``\\\"cpu\\\"`` if passed.\\n157: \\n158:         .. versionadded:: 2.0.0\\n159:     ${ARRAY_FUNCTION_LIKE}\\n160: \\n161:         .. versionadded:: 1.20.0\\n162: \\n163:     Returns\\n164:     -------\\n165:     out : ndarray\\n166:         Array of ones with the given shape, dtype, and order.\\n167: \\n168:     See Also\\n169:     --------\\n170:     ones_like : Return an array of ones with shape and type of input.\\n171:     empty : Return a new uninitialized array.\\n172:     zeros : Return a new array setting values to zero.\\n173:     full : Return a new array of given shape filled with value.\\n174: \\n175:     Examples\\n176:     --------\\n177:     >>> import numpy as np\\n178:     >>> np.ones(5)\\n179:     array([1., 1., 1., 1., 1.])\\n180: \\n181:     >>> np.ones((5,), dtype=int)\\n182:     array([1, 1, 1, 1, 1])\\n183: \\n184:     >>> np.ones((2, 1))\\n185:     array([[1.],\\n186:            [1.]])\\n187: \\n188:     >>> s = (2,2)\\n189:     >>> np.ones(s)\\n190:     array([[1.,  1.],\\n191:            [1.,  1.]])\\n192: \\n193:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n194:     if like is not None:\\n195:         return _ones_with_like(\\n196:             like, shape, dtype=dtype, order=order, device=device\\n197:         )\\n198: \\n199:     a = empty(shape, dtype, order, device=device)\\n200:     multiarray.copyto(a, 1, casting='unsafe')\\n201:     return a\\n202: \\n203: \\n204: _ones_with_like = array_function_dispatch()(ones)\\n205: \\n206: \\n207: def _ones_like_dispatcher(\\n208:     a, dtype=None, order=None, subok=None, shape=None, *, device=None\\n209: ):\\n210:     return (a,)\\n211: \\n212: \\n213: @array_function_dispatch(_ones_like_dispatcher)\\n214: def ones_like(\\n215:     a, dtype=None, order='K', subok=True, shape=None, *, device=None\\n216: ):\\n217:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n218:     Return an array of ones with the same shape and type as a given array.\\n219: \\n220:     Parameters\\n221:     ----------\\n222:     a : array_like\\n223:         The shape and data-type of `a` define these same attributes of\\n224:         the returned array.\\n225:     dtype : data-type, optional\\n226:         Overrides the data type of the result.\\n227:     order : {'C', 'F', 'A', or 'K'}, optional\\n228:         Overrides the memory layout of the result. 'C' means C-order,\\n229:         'F' means F-order, 'A' means 'F' if `a` is Fortran contiguous,\\n230:         'C' otherwise. 'K' means match the layout of `a` as closely\\n231:         as possible.\\n232:     subok : bool, optional.\\n233:         If True, then the newly created array will use the sub-class\\n234:         type of `a`, otherwise it will be a base-class array. Defaults\\n235:         to True.\\n236:     shape : int or sequence of ints, optional.\\n237:         Overrides the shape of the result. If order='K' and the number of\\n238:         dimensions is unchanged, will try to keep order, otherwise,\\n239:         order='C' is implied.\\n240:     device : str, optional\\n241:         The device on which to place the created array. Default: None.\\n242:         For Array-API interoperability only, so must be ``\\\"cpu\\\"`` if passed.\\n243: \\n244:         .. versionadded:: 2.0.0\\n245: \\n246:     Returns\\n247:     -------\\n248:     out : ndarray\\n249:         Array of ones with the same shape and type as `a`.\\n250: \\n251:     See Also\\n252:     --------\\n253:     empty_like : Return an empty array with shape and type of input.\\n254:     zeros_like : Return an array of zeros with shape and type of input.\\n255:     full_like : Return a new array with shape of input filled with value.\\n256:     ones : Return a new array setting values to one.\\n257: \\n258:     Examples\\n259:     --------\\n260:     >>> import numpy as np\\n261:     >>> x = np.arange(6)\\n262:     >>> x = x.reshape((2, 3))\\n263:     >>> x\\n264:     array([[0, 1, 2],\\n265:            [3, 4, 5]])\\n266:     >>> np.ones_like(x)\\n267:     array([[1, 1, 1],\\n268:            [1, 1, 1]])\\n269: \\n270:     >>> y = np.arange(3, dtype=float)\\n271:     >>> y\\n272:     array([0., 1., 2.])\\n273:     >>> np.ones_like(y)\\n274:     array([1.,  1.,  1.])\\n275: \\n276:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n277:     res = empty_like(\\n278:         a, dtype=dtype, order=order, subok=subok, shape=shape, device=device\\n279:     )\\n280:     multiarray.copyto(res, 1, casting='unsafe')\\n281:     return res\\n282: \\n283: @finalize_array_function_like\\n291: @set_module('numpy')\\n292: def full(shape, fill_value, dtype=None, order='C', *, device=None, like=None):\\n293:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n294:     Return a new array of given shape and type, filled with `fill_value`.\\n295: \\n296:     Parameters\\n297:     ----------\\n298:     shape : int or sequence of ints\\n299:         Shape of the new array, e.g., ``(2, 3)`` Output
"{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"Module containing non-deprecated functions borrowed from Numeric.\\n2: \\n3: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n4: import functools\\n5: import types\\n6: import warnings\\n7: \\n8: import numpy as np\\n9: from .._utils import set_module\\n10: from . import multiarray as mu\\n11: from . import overrides\\n12: from . import umath as um\\n13: from . import numerictypes as nt\\n14: from .multiarray import asarray, array, asanyarray, concatenate\\n15: from ._multiarray_umath import _array_converter\\n16: from . import _methods\\n17: \\n18: _dt_ = nt.sctype2char\\n19: \\n20: # functions that are methods\\n21: __all__ = [\\n22:     'all', 'amax', 'amin', 'any', 'argmax',\\n23:     'argmin', 'argpartition', 'argsort', 'around', 'choose', 'clip',\\n24:     'compress', 'cumprod', 'cumsum', 'cumulative_prod', 'cumulative_sum',\\n25:     'diagonal', 'mean', 'max', 'min', 'matrix_transpose',\\n26:     'ndim', 'nonzero', 'partition', 'prod', 'ptp', 'put',\\n27:     'ravel', 'repeat', 'reshape', 'resize', 'round',\\n28:     'searchsorted', 'shape', 'size', 'sort', 'squeeze',\\n29:     'std', 'sum', 'swapaxes', 'take', 'trace', 'transpose', 'var',\\n30: ]\\n31: \\n32: _gentype = types.GeneratorType\\n33: # save away Python sum\\n34: _sum_ = sum\\n35: \\n36: array_function_dispatch = functools.partial(\\n37:     overrides.array_function_dispatch, module='numpy')\\n38: \\n39: \\n40: # functions that are now methods\\n41: def _wrapit(obj, method, *args, **kwds):\\n42:     conv = _array_converter(obj)\\n43:     # As this already tried the method, subok is maybe quite reasonable here\\n44:     # but this follows what was done before. TODO: revisit this.\\n45:     arr, = conv.as_arrays(subok=False)\\n46:     result = getattr(arr, method)(*args, **kwds)\\n47: \\n48:     return conv.wrap(result, to_scalar=False)\\n49: \\n50: \\n51: def _wrapfunc(obj, method, *args, **kwds):\\n52:     bound = getattr(obj, method, None)\\n53:     if bound is None:\\n54:         return _wrapit(obj, method, *args, **kwds)\\n55: \\n56:     try:\\n57:         return bound(*args, **kwds)\\n58:     except TypeError:\\n59:         # A TypeError occurs if the object does have such a method in its\\n60:         # class, but its signature is not identical to that of NumPy's. This\\n61:         # situation has occurred in the case of a downstream library like\\n62:         # 'pandas'.\\n63:         #\\n64:         # Call _wrapit from within the except clause to ensure a potential\\n65:         # exception has a traceback chain.\\n66:         return _wrapit(obj, method, *args, **kwds)\\n67: \\n68: \\n69: def _wrapreduction(obj, ufunc, method, axis, dtype, out, **kwargs):\\n70:     passkwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items()\\n71:                   if v is not np._NoValue}\\n72: \\n73:     if type(obj) is not mu.ndarray:\\n74:         try:\\n75:             reduction = getattr(obj, method)\\n76:         except AttributeError:\\n77:             pass\\n78:         else:\\n79:             # This branch is needed for reductions like any which don't\\n80:             # support a dtype.\\n81:             if dtype is not None:\\n82:                 return reduction(axis=axis, dtype=dtype, out=out, **passkwargs)\\n83:             else:\\n84:                 return reduction(axis=axis, out=out, **passkwargs)\\n85: \\n86:     return ufunc.reduce(obj, axis, dtype, out, **passkwargs)\\n87: \\n88: \\n89: def _wrapreduction_any_all(obj, ufunc, method, axis, out, **kwargs):\\n90:     # Same as above function, but dtype is always bool (but never passed on)\\n91:     passkwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items()\\n92:                   if v is not np._NoValue}\\n93: \\n94:     if type(obj) is not mu.ndarray:\\n95:         try:\\n96:             reduction = getattr(obj, method)\\n97:         except AttributeError:\\n98:             pass\\n99:         else:\\n100:             return reduction(axis=axis, out=out, **passkwargs)\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/numpy--v2.2.2@9ca88bff35bf60de74f9f95e6f90e6b4e0913e78/-/blob/numpy/_core/fromnumeric.py%3FL1-100\"}"
